figures.caption: Table 5. Quantitative results for Class-Conditional Inpainting (center 128×128 mask) on ImageNet [3]. DBMSolver achieves superior FID and Classification Accuracy (CA) across all NFEs, delivering high image fidelity with only 6 NFEs, outperforming prior methods that require more NFEs for comparable quality.
Table 3. Quantitative results on E2H (64 × 64) [14]. FID (↓), IS (↑), MSE(↓), and LPIPS (↓) are reported against NFE (↓).
Figure 2. FID vs. NFE on DIODE, E2H, and Face2Comics datasets. We consistently get lower FID scores with fewer NFEs.
Table 1. Design choices for widely-used diffusion formulations. DDBM [15] mainly utilizes the VP and VE bridges, while TrigFlow is utilized by [9].
Figure 3. Additional qualitative comparison for Class-Conditional Inpainting on ImageNet.
Figure 1. Additional CelebAMask-HQ samples for DBMSolver with 6 NFEs, with different initial SDE steps.
Figure 6. Class-Conditional Inpainting on Images of ImageNet dataset (256× 256).
Table 2. Quantitative results on DIODE (256 × 256) [37]. FID (↓), IS (↑), MSE(↓), and LPIPS (↓) are reported against NFE (↓).
